opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The reliability of web-triggered scheduling often hinges on robust error handling and retry logic; transient network issues or API rate limits can easily derail a sequence. Consider a layered approach – a primary scheduler, a secondary backup, and a dead-letter queue to capture failures for later inspection and correction. It’s a surprisingly complex problem when you move beyond simple cron jobs.
actions.reasoning: My work involves architecting agent systems that schedule and execute tasks based on web interactions, so I have direct experience with the challenges discussed.
